title: Misatos Treatment of Shinji vs Asuka
display_title: Misatos Treatment of Shinji vs Asuka
wikitext:
[]: Misato's Treatment of Shinji vs. Asuka             Quote Post by Allemann » Thu Jul 28, 2011 7:54 am [Split from Favorite Character thread .] Hypocrisy and possible sexism: Misato slaps Shinji, an introverted, nonthreatening loser male, several times, whereas Asuka, a snotty fiery brat, gets a free ride with her egotistical temper tantrums.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jul 28, 2011 8:11 am Misato gives Shinji a metaphysical slap in EoE [/ambiguous], but that's it.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Thu Jul 28, 2011 8:31 am I clearly remember she slaped him after he told her something insolent. It was after an angle battle. He got reckless and got chewed on.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jul 28, 2011 8:35 am I just scanned the scene in question, and it appears that your memory has inserted a slap where none exist.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Thu Jul 28, 2011 9:07 am Well, it remains that Misato wasn't equally treating Shinji and Asuka.

[]:             Quote Post by BornIn1142 » Thu Jul 28, 2011 9:25 am Um, no kidding. Misato clearly cares much more about Shinji, at least if we're judging based on the amount of attention she gives him and the affection she tries to invest. The reason she chews out Shinji more is because she actually tries to raise him a bit, whereas she doesn't even bother to support Asuka (probably because she knows Asuka would snap back at her hard).

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jul 28, 2011 9:28 am Allemann wrote: Hypocrisy and possible sexism: Misato slaps Shinji, an introverted, nonthreatening loser male, several times, whereas Asuka, a snotty fiery brat, gets a free ride with her egotistical temper tantrums.

[]:             Quote Post by BiQ » Thu Jul 28, 2011 9:36 am Reichu wrote: I just scanned the scene in question, and it appears that your memory has inserted a slap where none exist.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Thu Jul 28, 2011 10:14 am When discussing Misato's different methods regarding Shinji and Asuka, it's worth remembering that Asuka didn't need any cajoling to get into her Eva. Shinji, by contrast, wasn't very motivated, to put it mildly, so Misato probably felt she had to use various strategems (ranging from guilt trips to coddling) to ensure that the kid will show up (most of the time). Of course, there is a more natural affection between Misato and Shinji, but when examining the dynamics of the Katsuragi household, one must remember that Misato's most important task is to make sure that the kids will pilot. Admittedly, she's doing a less than fantastic job. And i do agree that she (Misato) is a raging, shameless hypocrite on multiple levels, but hey, that's part of what makes her interesting.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jul 28, 2011 10:25 am SaltyJoe wrote: And i do agree that she (Misato) is a raging, shameless hypocrite on multiple levels, but hey, that's part of what makes her interesting.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Jul 28, 2011 10:41 am The point is that while Asuka is in as much a dire need of a hug as Shinji is, she doesn't show it. Misato simply fell for Asuka's illusion of self-suficientness untill it was too late, like almost everyone else did. Misato tried to get her to do teamwork(ep 9)/ is occasionally seen trying to motivate her (ep 10 and 12) and tries to "take her down a peg" in eps 12 and 16. (Witz disastrous result since Asuka's problems go beyond simple arrogance) She obviously thought that she was just a little full of herself until shit came down by ep 22... It's not that Misato "prefers" Shinji out of some sort of favoritism, it's that his problems are easy to see and closer to Misato's own, she just knows more about him and is thus able to emphasize with him/help him a tad better. Also, while Asuka superficially mantained a friedship of sorts with Misato, we find out that she disliked her all along, was jealous of her and cathegorically rejects any sort of mother figure cuz of trauma. In contrast to that, Shinji clings to anyone who offers him a little kindness to the point of occassionally comming off as pathetic. She wouldn't open up/send signals. She wouldn't let herself be parented, even if she, without question, needed it as much as Shinji. She had everyone fooled, not just Misato. There's that scene in Rebuild where they actually get to bond, and in it, Misato thanks her for doing the piloting thing for the first time, suggesting that it is only there that she realizes that she, unlike someone who is simply arrogant and quitev capable of praising themselves, needs to be thanked/ praised. (After Asuka, unlike her series counterpart, actually talked to Misato) Misato just happens to have more experience with Shinji's sort of problems, she's only human/not a professional shrink.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Thu Jul 28, 2011 10:58 am Kendrix wrote: She obviously thought that she was just a little full of herself until shit came down by ep 22...

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jul 28, 2011 11:16 am SaltyJoe wrote: In Episode 10, IIRC, Misato and Asuka had a short bit of dialog where it was confirmed that Misato knows about Asuka's past. Though Misato, like the kids she is entrusted with isn't exactly good at understanding people, i can't imagine that she didn't know that Asuka had some serious problems.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Thu Jul 28, 2011 1:54 pm BornIn1142 wrote: Um, no kidding. Misato clearly cares much more about Shinji, at least if we're judging based on the amount of attention she gives him and the affection she tries to invest. The reason she chews out Shinji more is because she actually tries to raise him a bit, whereas she doesn't even bother to support Asuka (probably because she knows Asuka would snap back at her hard).

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jul 28, 2011 2:06 pm Allemann wrote: There's duplicity in Misato's actions. Her intense involvement around Shinji, ranging from guilt trips to coddling as SaltyJoe says, results in psychological crises for poor old Shinji. In contrast, emotional negligence of Asuka had continued to uphold Asuka's positive self-image, which wasn't broken until the mind rape.

[]: Re: Misato's Treatment of Shinji vs. Asuka             Quote Post by CJD » Thu Jul 28, 2011 4:16 pm Allemann wrote: [Split from Favorite Character thread .] Hypocrisy and possible sexism: Misato slaps Shinji, an introverted, nonthreatening loser male, several times, whereas Asuka, a snotty fiery brat, gets a free ride with her egotistical temper tantrums.

[]: Re: Misato's Treatment of Shinji vs. Asuka             Quote Post by Tarnsman » Thu Jul 28, 2011 4:24 pm CJD wrote: The fact is Shinji and Asuka are two very different people, with different problems.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jul 28, 2011 4:40 pm BiQ wrote: In manga, Misato DOES slap Shinji after their shouting match after Shamshel fight. Relevance to series still zero, yes.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Thu Jul 28, 2011 5:06 pm Allemann wrote: There's duplicity in Misato's actions. Her intense involvement around Shinji, ranging from guilt trips to coddling as SaltyJoe says, results in psychological crises for poor old Shinji. In contrast, emotional negligence of Asuka had continued to uphold Asuka's positive self-image, which wasn't broken until the mind rape. The less Misato is a caretaker to a child, that child will be better off.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Jul 28, 2011 5:07 pm One difference in Misato's relationship with her two charges is that Shinji was a newcomer, whereas she already had a history with Asuka.
